title: Forkify Application Planning

learningObjectives.text: Apply feature decomposition using a new example and justify the result.
Explain project architecture planning using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain data and ui flow using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Forkify Application Planning is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Application Architecture: Forkify** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Feature decomposition** → **Project architecture planning** → **Data and UI flow**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Feature decomposition
sections.body: Start with user-visible outcomes and convert them into explicit behavior, inputs, edge cases, and acceptance conditions. Break large capabilities into end-to-end slices that can be implemented and verified independently.

sections.title: Project architecture planning
sections.body: Architecture planning identifies data, responsibilities, boundaries, and communication paths. The goal is to reduce costly ambiguity about ownership and change, not predict every future file.

sections.title: Data and UI flow
sections.body: Describe where data originates, who owns it, how it changes, and how the UI observes those changes. Explicit flow prevents hidden state and scattered updates.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.
summary: Forkify Application Planning connects Feature decomposition, Project architecture planning, Data and UI flow.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: A team is making a decision involving Forkify Application Planning. Which approach shows the strongest understanding of **Feature decomposition**?

choices.label: State the requirement and assumptions, apply the Feature decomposition model, and verify the observable consequence before adding complexity.
choices.feedback: Transferable understanding of Feature decomposition means using the model to make and verify a decision in a new context.

choices.label: Use the term Feature decomposition in documentation without changing any decision or behavior.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Choose the option with the most tools even when the requirement is smaller.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Transferable understanding of Feature decomposition means using the model to make and verify a decision in a new context.

questions.prompt: Which statement best distinguishes **Feature decomposition** from **Project architecture planning** in this topic?

choices.label: They address different parts of the topic's model; a correct decision identifies which one explains the current requirement and how the two interact.
choices.feedback: The concepts are related but should be applied according to the requirement and the role each plays in Forkify Application Planning.

choices.label: They are interchangeable labels, so choosing between them has no effect on reasoning.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Feature decomposition is always correct and Project architecture planning is always an error.

choices.label: The distinction matters only for the exact example used in the lesson.

questions.hint: Look for a distinction based on responsibilities or consequences, not an absolute rule.

questions.prompt: A developer notices this recurring mistake: “Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.” What is the best response?

choices.label: Return to the requirement, use the Forkify Application Planning model to identify the failed assumption, and verify the corrected behavior with a representative case.
choices.feedback: The useful response corrects both the misconception and the next development action for Forkify Application Planning.

choices.label: Hide the symptom and continue so the schedule is not affected.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Add unrelated abstractions before reproducing the problem.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Treat the mistake as unavoidable and remove the relevant check.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: The useful response corrects both the misconception and the next development action for Forkify Application Planning.
questions.hint: Choose the response that makes the failure reproducible and the correction verifiable.

questions.prompt: Which day-to-day workflow is most reliable when a task depends on Forkify Application Planning?

choices.label: Clarify the requirement, identify the relevant model or boundary, test the smallest representative case, then refactor after the behavior is understood.
choices.feedback: A reliable workflow makes assumptions and failures visible early, then improves the implementation after correctness is established.

choices.label: Start with the largest available abstraction and postpone defining expected behavior.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Copy a solution from another project without checking whether its assumptions match.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Optimize for line count first and inspect correctness only after release.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: A reliable workflow makes assumptions and failures visible early, then improves the implementation after correctness is established.
questions.hint: Prefer the sequence that produces evidence early and keeps decisions reversible.

questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best engineering judgment about Forkify Application Planning?

choices.label: Use Forkify Application Planning to reduce uncertainty: document the requirement, choose a proportionate approach, and leave evidence that another developer can review.
choices.feedback: Use Forkify Application Planning to reduce uncertainty: document the requirement, choose a proportionate approach, and leave evidence that another developer can review.

choices.label: Use Forkify Application Planning in every file because more abstraction always improves software.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Avoid Forkify Application Planning completely because every additional concept reduces maintainability.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Choose only by the number of lines and ignore correctness, ownership, and future change.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Forkify Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Use Forkify Application Planning to reduce uncertainty: document the requirement, choose a proportionate approach, and leave evidence that another developer can review.
questions.hint: Choose the option that balances correctness, clarity, cost, and the actual requirement.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.

assessmentProfile.label: Developer workflow and decision scenarios
assessmentProfile.rationale: The learner should make realistic development decisions using requirements, logs, file structures, configuration, or short code artifacts.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
